test_cases.title: Create regular vacation request — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with sufficient vacation days (>=5) in an office with AV=true or AV=false.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 5 AND e.enabled = true AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the selected employee
2. Navigate to Calendar of absences → My vacations and days off
3. Verify "My vacations and days off" page loads with Vacations tab active
4. Note the current "Available vacation days" count
5. Click "Create a request" button
6. Verify "Creating vacation request" dialog opens
7. Set start date to next Monday (DD.MM.YYYY format)
8. Set end date to next Friday
9. Verify "Number of days" auto-updates (should show 5 or 4 if holiday)
10. Verify "Unpaid vacation" checkbox is unchecked (Regular type)
11. Verify "Approved by" shows the employee's manager name
12. Verify "Vacation pay to be paid with salary for" shows a valid month
13. Click "Save" button
14. Verify success — dialog closes, vacation appears in the table
15. Verify new row shows Status = "New", Vacation type = "Regular"
16. Verify Regular days column shows the correct working day count
17. Verify Available vacation days count decreased accordingly
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation request created successfully.
Row appears in table with Status="New", type="Regular".
Available days decreased by the number of working days in the period.
Approved by shows the assigned manager.
test_cases.notes:
Number of days excludes weekends and office-specific public holidays.
For AV=true offices, full year balance is available from Jan 1.
For AV=false, monthly accrual applies.

test_cases.title: Create administrative (unpaid) vacation request
test_cases.preconditions:
Any enabled employee.
Query: SELECT login FROM ttt_vacation.employee WHERE enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to Calendar of absences → My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request" button
4. Set start date to a future Monday
5. Set end date to the same Monday (1-day vacation)
6. Check the "Unpaid vacation" checkbox
7. Verify "Number of days" shows 1
8. Click "Save" button
9. Verify vacation appears in table with type "Administrative"
10. Verify Administrative days = 1, Regular days = 0
11. Verify Available vacation days did NOT decrease
test_cases.expected_result:
Administrative vacation created. Type shows "Administrative".
No deduction from available vacation day balance.
Administrative days column shows the day count.
test_cases.notes: Administrative vacations have no balance requirement — can be created regardless of available days.

test_cases.title: Create vacation with comment
test_cases.preconditions: Enabled employee with sufficient days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Fill in valid start and end dates
5. Enter text in the "Comment" field: "Family trip"
6. Click "Save"
7. Verify vacation created successfully
8. Click "..." on the new vacation row to open Request details
9. Verify the comment is visible in the details dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Comment saved and visible in request details.
test_cases.notes: Comment field is optional. Verify it accepts special characters and multi-line text.

test_cases.title: Create vacation with "Also notify" colleagues
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with at least 2 colleagues in the same office.
Query: SELECT e1.login AS creator, e2.login AS colleague FROM ttt_vacation.employee e1 JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e2 ON e1.office_id = e2.office_id AND e1.id != e2.id WHERE e1.enabled = true AND e2.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Fill in valid start and end dates
5. In the "Also notify" multi-select field, search for a colleague name
6. Select 1-2 colleagues from the dropdown
7. Click "Save"
8. Verify vacation created successfully
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_notify_also WHERE vacation = <new_vacation_id>
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation created with notify-also recipients stored.
DB shows entries in vacation_notify_also table for each selected colleague.
test_cases.notes:
GET /vacations/{id} does NOT return notifyAlso data — must verify via DB.
The label has a typo: "Also notifty" instead of "Also notify".

test_cases.title: View vacation request details
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with at least one existing vacation (any status).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "..." action button on an existing vacation row
4. Verify "Request details" dialog opens
5. Verify the following fields are displayed:
   - Period (start — end date)
   - Number of days
   - Status (e.g., New, Approved, Paid)
   - Vacation type (Regular / Administrative)
   - Payment month
   - Approved by (manager name as link)
   - Agreed by (optional approvers)
6. Click "Close" button
7. Verify dialog closes, table is unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: Request details dialog shows all vacation attributes correctly.

test_cases.title: Edit vacation dates — NEW status
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW vacation request.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'NEW' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click edit (pencil icon) on the NEW vacation row
4. Verify edit dialog opens with pre-filled dates
5. Change start date to a different future Monday
6. Change end date to that Friday
7. Verify "Number of days" recalculates
8. Click "Save"
9. Verify vacation row updates with new dates
10. Verify status remains "New"
11. Verify Regular/Administrative days recalculated
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation dates updated. Status remains NEW.
Day count recalculated based on new date range.
test_cases.notes: Update validator does NOT check isNextVacationAvailable() — next year check is create-only.

test_cases.title: Edit APPROVED vacation — status resets to NEW
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND e.enabled = true AND v.start_date > CURRENT_DATE ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Locate the APPROVED vacation in the table
4. Click edit (pencil icon) on the row
5. Change end date to one day later
6. Click "Save"
7. Verify status changed from "Approved" to "New"
8. Verify the manager will need to re-approve
test_cases.expected_result:
Editing an APPROVED vacation resets status to NEW.
Requires re-approval from the manager.
Optional approvals also reset to ASKED status.
test_cases.notes: State transition: APPROVED → NEW on date edit. Days are recalculated.

test_cases.title: Verify vacation table columns and sorting
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with 3+ vacation records (mix of statuses).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "All" filter tab to see all vacations
4. Verify table columns: Vacation dates, Regular days, Administrative days, Vacation type, Approved by, Status, Payment month, Actions
5. Click "Vacation dates" column header to sort ascending
6. Verify rows are sorted by date ascending
7. Click again to sort descending
8. Verify rows reorder
9. Repeat sort test for Regular days, Status columns
test_cases.expected_result: All columns present. Sorting toggles between asc/desc on each click.

test_cases.title: Verify vacation table filters — status and type
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with vacations of different types and statuses.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "All" tab
3. Click filter icon on "Vacation type" column
4. Verify checkboxes: All, Regular, Administrative
5. Uncheck "All", check only "Administrative"
6. Verify table shows only Administrative vacations
7. Reset filter (check "All")
8. Click filter icon on "Status" column
9. Verify checkboxes: All, New, Approved, Rejected, Paid, Finished, Deleted
10. Check only "New" and "Approved"
11. Verify table shows only vacations with those statuses
test_cases.expected_result: Filters correctly restrict displayed rows. Checkboxes toggle independently.

test_cases.title: Verify Open/Closed/All filter tabs
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with both open (NEW/APPROVED) and closed (PAID/CANCELED) vacations.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Verify "Open" tab is active by default
4. Verify only NEW and APPROVED vacations shown
5. Click "Closed" tab
6. Verify only PAID, CANCELED, REJECTED, DELETED vacations shown
7. Click "All" tab
8. Verify all vacations shown regardless of status
test_cases.expected_result: Tab filters correctly segment vacations by status category.
test_cases.notes: Open = NEW + APPROVED. Closed = PAID + CANCELED + REJECTED + DELETED + FINISHED.

test_cases.title: Verify available vacation days display and yearly breakdown
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacation balance in current and past years.
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Verify "Available vacation days: N in YYYY" displayed
4. Click the info (i) icon next to the year
5. Verify popup shows per-year breakdown (e.g., 2025: 10, 2026: 24)
6. Verify total matches the main display
7. Close the popup
test_cases.expected_result:
Available days shows correct total.
Yearly breakdown popup shows remaining days per year.
DB-CHECK: SELECT year, available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.login = '<login>'
test_cases.notes: AV=true: full year balance available. AV=false: monthly accrual.

test_cases.title: Verify total row in vacation table
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with 2+ vacations visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "All" tab
3. Scroll to table footer
4. Verify "Total" row exists
5. Verify it sums Regular days column
6. Verify it sums Administrative days column
7. Compare with manual sum of visible rows
test_cases.expected_result: Total row accurately sums Regular and Administrative days columns.

test_cases.title: Create vacation starting today
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with sufficient days. Today must be a weekday.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Set start date to today's date
5. Set end date to today
6. Verify "Number of days" shows 1 (if today is a working day)
7. Click "Save"
8. Verify vacation created with status "New"
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation with start date = today is accepted (boundary: today is valid, yesterday is not).
test_cases.notes: Backend validation: isBefore(today) — today itself passes. Past dates fail.

test_cases.title: Create cross-year vacation (December to January)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with sufficient days in current and next year.
SETUP: If current month is Jan-Oct, set server clock to late November via test API:
PATCH /api/ttt/test/v1/clock with offset to reach Nov 15
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Set start date to Dec 22 of current year
5. Set end date to Jan 9 of next year
6. Verify "Number of days" calculates (excludes weekends and holidays in both years)
7. Click "Save"
8. Verify vacation created successfully
9. Verify the day split between current and next year in Available days popup
test_cases.expected_result:
Cross-year vacation created. Days split between Dec and Jan.
FIFO distribution: Dec days from current year balance, Jan days from next year.
test_cases.notes: Cross-year vacations consume days from both year buckets. Uses FIFO distribution.

test_cases.title: Verify payment month auto-calculation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with sufficient days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Set start date to a future date (e.g., 2 weeks from now)
5. Set end date to start + 4 days
6. Observe "Vacation pay to be paid with salary for" field
7. Verify it auto-populates with the month containing the start date
8. Try changing to a different month
9. Verify the system accepts or adjusts the payment month
test_cases.expected_result: Payment month auto-calculated based on vacation start date. Can be adjusted by user.
test_cases.notes: Backend: correctPaymentMonth() adjusts if needed. isPaymentDateCorrect() validates.

test_cases.title: Verify "Number of days" auto-calculation in dialog
test_cases.preconditions: Employee. Know the office production calendar for target dates.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to a Monday
4. Set end date to Friday of the same week
5. Verify "Number of days" shows 5 (or 4 if a public holiday falls in the range)
6. Change end date to Saturday
7. Verify day count still shows 5 (Saturday excluded)
8. Change start date to Saturday, end date to Sunday
9. Verify day count shows 0
test_cases.expected_result:
Day count dynamically updates as dates change.
Excludes weekends and office-specific public holidays.
Sat-Sun only range shows 0 days.
test_cases.notes: Uses VacationDaysCalculatorImpl with office production calendar.

test_cases.title: Create vacation with optional approvers
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who is NOT a CPO/DM. Has a manager.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er WHERE er.employee_id = e.cs_id AND er.role = 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER') ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Fill in valid dates
5. Verify "Approved by" shows the direct manager
6. Note whether "Agreed by" section is empty or pre-filled
7. Click "Save"
8. Verify vacation created
DB-CHECK: SELECT va.status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_approval va WHERE va.vacation = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation created with manager as primary approver. Optional approval entries in DB if applicable.
test_cases.notes: CPO path: self-approval + manager as optional approver (ASKED). Regular: manager approves.

test_cases.title: CPO creates vacation — self-approval
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER (CPO) who has a manager.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.cs_id = er.employee_id WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER' AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the CPO employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Fill in valid dates
5. Verify "Approved by" shows the CPO themselves (self-approval)
6. Verify "Agreed by" shows the CPO's manager (as optional approver)
7. Click "Save"
8. Verify vacation created with status "New"
DB-CHECK: SELECT v.approver_id, e.login FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.approver_id = e.id WHERE v.id = <id>
DB-CHECK: SELECT va.employee, va.status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_approval va WHERE va.vacation = <id>
test_cases.expected_result:
CPO is self-approver. Manager appears as optional approver with ASKED status.
Approver ID in vacation table = CPO's own employee ID.
test_cases.notes: VacationServiceImpl: if isCPO → setApproverId(employee.getId()), manager added as optional.

test_cases.title: Verify pagination on vacation table
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with >10 vacation records.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "All" tab
3. Verify pagination controls at bottom of table
4. Click page 2 button
5. Verify table shows next page of results
6. Click "Previous" button
7. Verify returns to page 1
8. Click "Next" button
9. Verify moves to page 2 again
test_cases.expected_result: Pagination works correctly. Page numbers and prev/next buttons navigate between pages.

test_cases.title: Verify vacation events feed
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with vacation history (create, approve, cancel events).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Vacation events feed" button (calendar icon)
4. Verify events feed opens/displays
5. Verify events show vacation lifecycle actions (created, approved, etc.)
6. Verify events are ordered chronologically
test_cases.expected_result: Events feed displays vacation lifecycle events in chronological order.
test_cases.notes: Timeline events: VACATION_CREATED, VACATION_APPROVED, VACATION_REJECTED, etc.

test_cases.title: Cancel NEW vacation request
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'NEW' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Locate the NEW vacation in the table
4. Click action menu (three-dot or action icon) on the row
5. Click "Cancel" option
6. Verify confirmation dialog (if any)
7. Confirm the cancellation
8. Verify vacation status changes to "Canceled"
9. Verify vacation moves to "Closed" tab
10. Verify available days restored to previous value
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation status = Canceled. Days returned to balance.
Vacation visible under "Closed" tab.
test_cases.notes: State transition: NEW → CANCELED (ROLE_EMPLOYEE).

test_cases.title: Cancel APPROVED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED future vacation (payment date after current report period).
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND v.start_date > CURRENT_DATE AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Locate the APPROVED vacation
4. Click cancel action on the row
5. Confirm the cancellation
6. Verify status changes to "Canceled"
7. Verify available vacation days increase back
8. Verify the row moves to "Closed" tab
test_cases.expected_result:
APPROVED vacation canceled. Status = Canceled.
Days returned to balance. FIFO redistribution triggered.
test_cases.notes: Guarded by canBeCancelled: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate → blocked.

test_cases.title: Restore CANCELED vacation (re-open)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a CANCELED vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'CANCELED' AND v.start_date > CURRENT_DATE AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "Closed" tab
3. Locate the CANCELED vacation
4. Click edit (pencil icon) or restore action
5. Verify edit dialog opens with the original dates
6. Click "Save" without changes (or adjust dates)
7. Verify status changes back to "New"
8. Verify vacation moves to "Open" tab
9. Verify available days decrease again
test_cases.expected_result:
CANCELED → NEW transition works. Vacation re-opened.
Days recalculated and deducted from balance.
test_cases.notes: Explicit transition in VacationStatusManager: CANCELED → NEW (ROLE_EMPLOYEE). Despite CANCELED being in FINAL_STATUSES.

test_cases.title: Delete NEW vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a NEW vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Locate the NEW vacation
4. Click delete action (trash icon or menu option)
5. Confirm deletion
6. Verify vacation disappears from "Open" tab
7. Switch to "All" tab
8. Verify vacation shows with status "Deleted"
9. Verify available days restored
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation soft-deleted. Status = Deleted. Days returned to balance.
test_cases.notes: Soft delete — record persists with status=DELETED. DELETED records create ghost conflicts for future vacations at same dates.

test_cases.title: Cannot delete PAID vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a PAID+EXACT vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'PAID' AND v.period_type = 'EXACT' AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "Closed" tab
3. Locate the PAID vacation
4. Verify no delete/cancel action buttons are available
5. Verify only "Request details" (view) action is available
test_cases.expected_result: No delete/cancel actions for PAID vacations. Permission service returns empty set.
test_cases.notes: Guard: PAID+EXACT → ServiceException("exception.vacation.delete.notAllowed"). VacationPermissionService returns no permissions for PAID status.

test_cases.title: Cannot cancel PAID vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a PAID vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "Closed" tab
3. Locate the PAID vacation
4. Verify no cancel action is available
5. Verify the row shows only a view details button
test_cases.expected_result: PAID is a terminal state. No status-changing actions available in UI.
test_cases.notes: PAID status: VacationPermissionService.calculate() returns empty set → no action buttons rendered.

test_cases.title: Cannot cancel APPROVED vacation after accounting period close
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED REGULAR vacation with payment date before current office report period.
SETUP: Use test API to advance clock so reportPeriod is after the vacation paymentDate.
PATCH /api/ttt/test/v1/clock to set date past the payment month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Locate the APPROVED vacation whose payment month has passed
4. Verify no cancel/delete action buttons are available for this vacation
5. Verify only view details is available
test_cases.expected_result: canBeCancelled returns false: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate → actions disabled.
test_cases.notes: The canBeCancelled guard protects closed accounting periods from cancellation.

test_cases.title: Verify days returned to balance after cancel
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with known available days and a NEW REGULAR 5-day vacation.
Note the available days before and after creating the vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Note the "Available vacation days" count (call it X)
4. Cancel the 5-day NEW vacation
5. Verify "Available vacation days" increases back to X
6. Verify the days were returned correctly
DB-CHECK: SELECT SUM(available_vacation_days) FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.login = '<login>'
test_cases.expected_result: Available days fully restored after cancellation. FIFO redistribution may adjust other vacations.
test_cases.notes: VacationRecalculationService.recalculate() triggered on cancel. FIFO redistribution runs.

test_cases.title: Delete REJECTED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a REJECTED vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'REJECTED' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "Closed" tab
3. Locate the REJECTED vacation
4. Click delete action
5. Confirm deletion
6. Verify status changes to "Deleted"
test_cases.expected_result: REJECTED vacation can be deleted. Status = Deleted.
test_cases.notes: REJECTED is deletable because it is not PAID.

test_cases.title: Delete CANCELED vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a CANCELED vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off → "Closed" tab
3. Locate the CANCELED vacation
4. Click delete action
5. Confirm deletion
6. Verify status changes to "Deleted"
7. Verify vacation remains visible under "All" tab with status "Deleted"
test_cases.expected_result: CANCELED vacation deleted (soft delete). Status = Deleted.

test_cases.title: Approve NEW vacation request (manager view)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with pending approval requests.
Query: SELECT m.login AS manager, e.login AS employee, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON v.approver_id = m.id WHERE v.status = 'NEW' AND m.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Calendar of absences → Employees requests
3. Verify "Vacation requests" tab is active
4. Click "Approval" sub-tab
5. Locate the employee's NEW vacation request in the table
6. Click approve button (checkmark icon, data-testid="vacation-request-action-approve")
7. Verify confirmation prompt (if any)
8. Confirm approval
9. Verify vacation disappears from Approval list (or status updates)
10. Login as the employee to verify status = "Approved" in My Vacations
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation status changes to APPROVED.
Request removed from manager's pending approval list.
Employee sees status "Approved" in their vacation table.
test_cases.notes: State: NEW → APPROVED. Days deducted at approval. Payment date may be adjusted to approvePeriodStartDate.

test_cases.title: Reject NEW vacation request (manager view)
test_cases.preconditions: Manager with pending NEW vacation requests.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Employees requests → Vacation requests → Approval
3. Locate a NEW vacation request
4. Click reject button (X icon, data-testid="vacation-request-action-reject")
5. Verify rejection dialog opens (may ask for reason)
6. Confirm rejection
7. Verify vacation disappears from pending list
8. Login as the employee
9. Verify vacation status = "Rejected" in My Vacations → Closed tab
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation rejected. Status = REJECTED. Employee sees it under Closed tab.
test_cases.notes: State: NEW → REJECTED (PM, DM, ADMIN). Days returned.

test_cases.title: Re-approve REJECTED vacation without edit
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a REJECTED vacation they can approve.
Note: No REJECTED→NEW transition exists — direct REJECTED→APPROVED is the only re-approval path.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager/approver
2. Navigate to Employees requests
3. Find the REJECTED vacation (may need to check "All" or specific filter)
4. Click approve button
5. Confirm approval
6. Verify status changes directly from REJECTED to APPROVED
7. Login as the employee to verify
test_cases.expected_result: Direct REJECTED → APPROVED transition. No edit required.
test_cases.notes: VacationStatusManager has explicit REJECTED→APPROVED entry. No REJECTED→NEW exists.

test_cases.title: Reject APPROVED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with an APPROVED vacation they approved (canBeCancelled = true).
Query: SELECT m.login AS manager, v.id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON v.approver_id = m.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND v.start_date > CURRENT_DATE AND m.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Employees requests
3. Find the APPROVED vacation
4. Click reject button
5. Confirm rejection
6. Verify status changes to REJECTED
7. Verify days returned to employee's balance
test_cases.expected_result: APPROVED → REJECTED. Days returned to pool. FIFO redistribution triggered.
test_cases.notes: REJECTABLE_STATUSES = {NEW, APPROVED}. canBeCancelled guard applies.

test_cases.title: Redirect vacation request to another manager
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a pending vacation request.
Another manager exists to redirect to.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the current approver
2. Navigate to Employees requests → Approval
3. Find a NEW vacation request
4. Click redirect button (arrow icon, data-testid="vacation-request-action-redirect")
5. Verify redirect dialog opens
6. Search for and select another manager
7. Confirm redirect
8. Verify vacation disappears from current manager's list
9. Login as the new approver
10. Verify vacation appears in their Approval list
test_cases.expected_result:
Vacation request redirected to new approver.
Old approver no longer sees it. New approver sees it in their queue.
test_cases.notes: Uses PUT /change-approver endpoint. Known bug: pass endpoint NPE on qa-1 (Caffeine cache).

test_cases.title: Approve vacation — verify days deducted
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager + employee pair. Know employee available days before approval.
Employee has a 5-day NEW vacation pending approval.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and note "Available vacation days" (call it X)
3. Logout
4. Login as the manager
5. Navigate to Employees requests → Approval
6. Approve the employee's vacation
7. Logout
8. Login as the employee again
9. Navigate to My vacations
10. Verify "Available vacation days" is approximately X (days consumed at create, confirmed at approve)
11. Verify vacation status = "Approved"
test_cases.expected_result: Days are deducted at creation, confirmed at approval. Balance reflects approved vacation.
test_cases.notes: Days consumed at creation via FIFO. Approval confirms the deduction.

test_cases.title: Reject vacation — verify days returned
test_cases.preconditions: Manager + employee pair. Employee has a NEW vacation pending approval.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee, note available days (call it X)
2. Logout and login as the manager
3. Reject the employee's vacation
4. Logout and login as the employee
5. Verify available days returned to X + vacation days
6. Verify vacation status = "Rejected" under Closed tab
test_cases.expected_result: Rejection returns days to pool. Available days increase back.
test_cases.notes: FIFO redistribution triggered: days returned may be redistributed to remaining vacations.

test_cases.title: Pay APPROVED vacation (accountant view)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with accountant role (ROLE_ACCOUNTANT or ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT).
An APPROVED vacation exists for an employee.
Query: SELECT v.id, e.login FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND v.period_type = 'EXACT' AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting → Vacation payment (or similar menu)
3. Verify payment table loads with pending vacations
4. Locate the APPROVED vacation
5. Click pay action / payment button
6. Verify payment dialog opens
7. Verify regular days and administrative days fields show correct values
8. Confirm payment
9. Verify vacation status changes to "Paid"
10. Verify vacation disappears from pending payment list
test_cases.expected_result:
APPROVED → PAID transition. Payment record created.
Vacation_payment record stored in DB.
test_cases.notes:
PayVacationServiceImpl validates: status must be APPROVED, periodType must be EXACT.
regularDaysPayed + administrativeDaysPayed must equal vacation.days.

test_cases.title: Pay administrative vacation

test_cases.expected_result: Administrative vacation paid. No balance impact. DB shows regular_days=0.
test_cases.notes: ADMINISTRATIVE pay: regularDaysPayed=0, administrativeDaysPayed=N.

test_cases.title: Auto-pay expired approved vacations
test_cases.preconditions:
An APPROVED vacation with start_date > 2 months in the past.
SETUP: Advance clock via test API to make the vacation old enough.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Create and approve a vacation for past dates via UI or API
2. SETUP: Advance server clock by 2+ months: PATCH /api/ttt/test/v1/clock
3. SETUP: Trigger auto-pay job: POST /api/vacation/v1/test/vacations/pay-expired-approved
4. Login as the employee
5. Navigate to My vacations → "Closed" tab
6. Verify the vacation status is now "Paid"
DB-CHECK: SELECT v.status, vp.payed_at FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v LEFT JOIN ttt_vacation.vacation_payment vp ON v.vacation_payment_id = vp.id WHERE v.id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result:
VacationStatusUpdateJob auto-pays expired approved vacations.
Payment distributed: regular days + administrative days.
test_cases.notes: payExpiredApproved() runs daily. Finds APPROVED >2 months old. Auto-distributes payment by type.

test_cases.title: Verify payment record in DB after payment
test_cases.preconditions: Just-paid vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. After paying a vacation (via TC-VAC-048), note the vacation ID
DB-CHECK: SELECT vp.id, vp.regular_days, vp.administrative_days, vp.payed_at FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.vacation_payment vp ON v.vacation_payment_id = vp.id WHERE v.id = <vacation_id>
2. Verify regular_days matches the vacation's regular working days
3. Verify administrative_days matches the vacation's admin days
4. Verify payed_at is today (or the specified date)
test_cases.expected_result: vacation_payment record created with correct day split and payment date.
test_cases.notes: FK: vacation.vacation_payment_id → vacation_payment.id. NOT a shared PK.

test_cases.expected_result: Bulk payment processes multiple vacations at once.

test_cases.title: Verify available days for AV=false employee (monthly accrual)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=false office.
Query: SELECT e.login, o.name FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the AV=false employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Read "Available vacation days" count
4. Calculate expected: (current_month * annual_norm / 12) + adjustments
5. Click info icon for yearly breakdown
6. Verify the balance matches monthly accrual model
DB-CHECK: SELECT ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.login = '<login>'
test_cases.expected_result: AV=false: days accrue monthly. Balance = (month * norm/12) + past + edited - consumed.
test_cases.notes: AV=false offices (Russia): monthly accrual. Never goes negative.

test_cases.title: Verify available days for AV=true employee (full year)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=true office.
Query: SELECT e.login, o.name FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the AV=true employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Read "Available vacation days" count
4. Verify full annual norm is available from January 1
5. Click info icon to verify yearly breakdown
6. Verify balance can be fractional (e.g., 20.193)
DB-CHECK: Same as TC-VAC-056
test_cases.expected_result: AV=true: full year balance available from Jan 1. Can go negative. Fractional display.
test_cases.notes: AV=true offices (Cyprus, Germany): advance vacation. Negative balance allowed.

test_cases.title: Verify FIFO day consumption (earliest year first)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with remaining days in both past year and current year.
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.enabled = true AND ev.available_vacation_days > 0 GROUP BY e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Note per-year breakdown (click info icon)
3. Create a vacation that consumes days (e.g., 3 days)
4. After creation, check per-year breakdown again
5. Verify days were consumed from the earliest year first
DB-CHECK: SELECT year, available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.login = '<login>' ORDER BY year
test_cases.expected_result: FIFO: days consumed from earliest year bucket first. Only when exhausted, next year used.
test_cases.notes: vacation_days_distribution tracks per-vacation year split.

test_cases.title: Verify working days exclude holidays
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee. Know the office production calendar — identify a week with a public holiday.
Query: SELECT cd.date, cd.day_type FROM ttt_calendar.calendar_day cd JOIN ttt_calendar.calendar c ON cd.calendar_id = c.id WHERE c.office_id = <office_id> AND cd.day_type != 'WORKING' AND cd.date > CURRENT_DATE LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Set start date to the Monday of the week with a public holiday
5. Set end date to the Friday of that week
6. Verify "Number of days" shows 4 (not 5) because of the holiday
7. Cancel the dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Working day count excludes office-specific public holidays. Mon-Fri with 1 holiday = 4 days.
test_cases.notes: VacationDaysCalculatorImpl uses office production calendar.

test_cases.title: Verify administrative vacation does not deduct days

AV=false employee with very few available days (e.g., 1-2 days remaining).

6. Verify bar starts and ends on correct days
7. Verify weekends within the range are marked differently
test_cases.expected_result: Green vacation bar correctly spans the vacation start-to-end dates on the chart.
test_cases.notes: Only APPROVED and PAID vacations shown on chart (not NEW or REJECTED).

test_cases.title: Employee can view own vacations only
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee (ROLE_EMPLOYEE only, no manager roles).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.cs_id = er.employee_id WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_EMPLOYEE' AND e.enabled = true AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er2 WHERE er2.employee_id = e.cs_id AND er2.role IN ('ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER','ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER','ROLE_ADMIN')) LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as a regular employee
2. Verify "My vacations and days off" page accessible
3. Verify the table shows ONLY own vacations
4. Navigate to Calendar of absences menu
5. Verify "Employees requests" menu item is NOT visible
6. Attempt direct navigation to /vacation/request
7. Verify access denied or redirect
test_cases.expected_result: Regular employee sees only own vacations. No access to Employee Requests page.
test_cases.notes: VACATIONS:VIEW_APPROVES required for /vacation/request. Only PM, DM, TL, ADMIN, VALL have it.

test_cases.title: Manager can view and act on Employee Requests
test_cases.preconditions: User with ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the project manager
2. Navigate to Calendar of absences → Employees requests
3. Verify page loads with pending requests
4. Verify approve/reject/redirect buttons visible for subordinate requests
5. Verify requests from non-subordinates are NOT shown in Approval tab
test_cases.expected_result: Manager sees only requests they are approver for. Actions available for those requests.

test_cases.title: Accountant can access Payment page
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_ACCOUNTANT.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.cs_id = er.employee_id WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_ACCOUNTANT' AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant
2. Verify "Accounting" menu visible in navigation
3. Navigate to Accounting → Vacation payment
4. Verify payment table loads
5. Verify pay action buttons visible for APPROVED vacations
test_cases.expected_result: Accountant has full access to vacation payment page and actions.
test_cases.notes: VACATIONS:VIEW_PAYMENTS required. ACC, CACC, ADMIN, VALL have it.

test_cases.title: Regular employee cannot access Payment page
test_cases.preconditions: Regular employee without accountant role.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as regular employee
2. Verify "Accounting" menu is NOT visible in navigation
3. Attempt direct navigation to /vacation/payment
4. Verify access denied or redirect
test_cases.expected_result: Payment page inaccessible to non-accountant users.

test_cases.title: ReadOnly user cannot create vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with readOnly = true.
Query: SELECT login FROM ttt_vacation.employee WHERE read_only = true AND enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the readOnly employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Verify "Create a request" button is disabled or hidden
4. If button visible, click it and verify creation is blocked
5. Verify no edit/cancel/delete actions on existing vacations
test_cases.expected_result: ReadOnly users cannot create, edit, cancel, or delete vacations.
test_cases.notes: VacationPermissionService: readOnly → returns empty permissions set.

test_cases.title: Non-approver cannot approve/reject vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Two managers: Manager A is the approver, Manager B is not.
A NEW vacation request assigned to Manager A.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as Manager B (who is NOT the approver)
2. Navigate to Employees requests
3. Verify the vacation request does NOT appear in Manager B's Approval list
4. (If visible in another tab) Verify no approve/reject buttons for this vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Only the assigned approver can approve/reject. Other managers cannot act on it.
test_cases.notes: hasAccess() checks approverId matches currentEmployee.id for manager roles.

test_cases.title: Verify permissions for NEW vacation (owner)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a NEW vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations
3. Verify action buttons on the NEW vacation row:
   - Edit (pencil icon) — available
   - Cancel — available
   - Delete — available
4. Verify no approve/pay buttons visible (owner cannot self-approve unless CPO)
test_cases.expected_result: Owner of NEW vacation: EDIT, CANCEL, DELETE available. No APPROVE/PAY.
test_cases.notes: VacationPermissionService: owner gets EDIT, DELETE, CANCEL for non-PAID status.

test_cases.title: Verify permissions for APPROVED vacation (owner)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with an APPROVED vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to My vacations
3. Verify action buttons on the APPROVED vacation row:
   - Edit — available (will reset to NEW)
   - Cancel — available (if canBeCancelled)
   - Delete — available (if canBeCancelled)
4. Verify no approve/pay buttons visible
test_cases.expected_result: Owner of APPROVED: EDIT, CANCEL (conditional), DELETE (conditional) available.
test_cases.notes: canBeCancelled guard: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate → cancel/delete blocked.

test_cases.title: Admin role — full access across pages
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_ADMIN.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.cs_id = er.employee_id WHERE er.role = 'ROLE_ADMIN' AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Verify all menu items visible: My vacations, Employee Requests, Availability chart, Vacation days, Payment, Day correction
3. Navigate to each page and verify access
4. Verify admin can see all employees' data
5. Note: admin transition map entry exists but hasAccess() bug may block some operations
test_cases.expected_result: Admin has access to all vacation-related pages.
test_cases.notes: Known bug: ROLE_ADMIN in transition map but NOT in MANAGER_ROLES → hasAccess() returns false for admin-as-approver.

test_cases.title: Start date in the past — error message
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee with vacation days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My vacations and days off
3. Click "Create a request"
4. Set start date to yesterday
5. Set end date to tomorrow
6. Click "Save"
7. Verify error message about start date in the past
8. Verify vacation is NOT created
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: validation.vacation.start.date.in.past
Note: i18n key may display as raw string (known bug — missing translation).
test_cases.notes: Backend: isBefore(today). Today is valid, yesterday is not. Frontend check may also catch this.

test_cases.title: End date before start date — error message
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to next Friday
4. Set end date to next Monday (before start)
5. Click "Save"
6. Verify error message about dates order
7. Verify vacation is NOT created
test_cases.expected_result: Error: validation.vacation.dates.order. May show as raw i18n key.
test_cases.notes: Frontend Formik validation may also block before reaching backend.

test_cases.title: Next year vacation before Feb 1 — error
test_cases.preconditions:
Current date is before February 1 of the current year.
SETUP: Set server clock to January 15: PATCH /api/ttt/test/v1/clock
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to January of NEXT year
4. Set end date to next year as well
5. Click "Save"
6. Verify error: validation.vacation.next.year.not.available
test_cases.expected_result: Cannot create vacation in next year before Feb 1 of current year.
test_cases.notes: VacationCreateValidator: startDate.year > today.year AND today < Feb 1.

test_cases.title: Vacation with 0 working days (Sat-Sun only) — duration error
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to a Saturday
4. Set end date to the next Sunday
5. Verify "Number of days" shows 0
6. Click "Save"
7. Verify error about minimum vacation duration
test_cases.expected_result: Error: validation.vacation.duration — 0 working days below minimum (1).
test_cases.notes: minimalVacationDuration = 1 (not 5 as Javadoc says). Only 0-day ranges trigger this.

test_cases.title: Overlapping vacation dates — crossing error
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing vacation (any status including DELETED).
Know the date range of the existing vacation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set dates that overlap with an existing vacation
4. Click "Save"
5. Verify error message about crossing/overlapping vacations
6. Verify vacation is NOT created
test_cases.expected_result:
Error: exception.validation.vacation.dates.crossing
Note: crossing check includes DELETED records (ghost conflicts).
test_cases.notes: Known issue: soft-deleted vacations create permanent ghost conflicts blocking those dates.

test_cases.title: Multiple validation errors displayed simultaneously
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to yesterday (past date)
4. Set end date to the day before yesterday (end < start AND in past)
5. Click "Save"
6. Verify BOTH error messages displayed:
   - Start date in the past
   - End date before start date
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple validation errors shown simultaneously (non-short-circuiting).
test_cases.notes: Past-date and dates-order checks run independently. Both errors returned together.

test_cases.title: Null payment month — server error (known bug)
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee. Test requires bypassing frontend validation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Fill valid dates
4. Clear the payment month field (if possible via UI)
5. Click "Save"
6. If frontend prevents empty payment month, test via API:
   POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with paymentMonth: null
7. Verify HTTP 500 (NPE)
test_cases.expected_result: Known bug: null paymentMonth causes NPE (HTTP 500). No @NotNull on DTO field.
test_cases.notes: Bug: paymentMonth has no @NotNull annotation. REGULAR type → correctPaymentMonth() NPEs.

test_cases.title: Same-day vacation (start = end, weekday)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with sufficient days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date = end date = a future weekday
4. Verify "Number of days" = 1
5. Click "Save"
6. Verify vacation created with Regular days = 1
test_cases.expected_result: Single-day vacation accepted. 1 working day counted.
test_cases.notes: Minimum working days = 1. Single weekday passes validation.

test_cases.title: Long vacation (30+ calendar days)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with 20+ available days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set start date to a future Monday
4. Set end date 30+ calendar days later
5. Verify "Number of days" shows working days (approximately 22)
6. Click "Save"
7. Verify vacation created if sufficient balance
test_cases.expected_result: Long vacations accepted if employee has sufficient available days.
test_cases.notes: No maximum duration limit exists. Only minimum (1 working day) enforced.

test_cases.title: Vacation before 3-month employment check
test_cases.preconditions:
New employee hired less than 3 months ago.
Query: SELECT login, first_date FROM ttt_vacation.employee WHERE first_date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' AND enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the new employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set dates within 3 months of employment start
4. Click "Save"
5. Verify error: exception.validation.vacation.too.early
test_cases.expected_result: Error: vacation days under limitation date exceed limit for recent hires.
test_cases.notes: DaysLimitationService uses employee.first_date for 3-month restriction.

test_cases.title: Special characters in comment field
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Fill valid dates
4. In Comment field, enter: <script>alert("XSS")</script> & "quotes" 'single'
5. Click "Save"
6. Verify vacation created (or safely rejected)
7. View the vacation details
8. Verify no script execution — text displayed as-is or sanitized
test_cases.expected_result: Special characters handled safely. No XSS execution. Text escaped or sanitized.

test_cases.title: Insufficient days for REGULAR vacation (AV=false)
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=false employee with 0 or very few available days.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND e.enabled = true AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY e.login HAVING SUM(ev.available_vacation_days) < 3 LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the AV=false employee
2. Click "Create a request"
3. Set dates for 5 working days (exceeding available balance)
4. Click "Save"
5. Verify error about insufficient vacation days
test_cases.expected_result: Error: validation.vacation.duration — availablePaidDays < total.
test_cases.notes: AV=false cannot go negative. AV=true CAN create vacation exceeding balance.

test_cases.title: Verify i18n missing keys displayed as raw strings (known bug)
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee. English language selected.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Ensure language is set to English (EN)
3. Trigger a validation error (e.g., past date)
4. Verify error message displays as raw i18n key:
   "validation.vacation.start.date.in.past" instead of human-readable text
test_cases.expected_result: Known bug: 3 validation keys have no i18n translations — displayed as raw key strings.
test_cases.notes: Missing translations: validation.vacation.start.date.in.past, validation.vacation.dates.order, validation.vacation.next.year.not.available.

test_cases.title: Email notification on vacation create (to approver)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a manager. Email service configured.
SETUP: Clear email queue: POST /api/email/test/v1/del-emails
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Clear email queue via test API
2. Login as the employee
3. Create a new vacation request
4. SETUP: Trigger email sending: POST /api/email/test/v1/send-emails
5. Verify email sent to the approver (manager)
6. Check email content includes: employee name, vacation dates, type
API-CHECK: GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<manager_email>
test_cases.expected_result: Email notification sent to approver when vacation created.
test_cases.notes: VacationCreatedEvent triggers notification. Use email test API to verify.

test_cases.title: Email notification on vacation approve (to employee)
test_cases.preconditions: Manager with a pending NEW vacation to approve. Email queue cleared.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Clear email queue
2. Login as the manager and approve the vacation
3. SETUP: Trigger email sending
4. Verify email sent to the employee
5. Check email content: vacation dates, new status "Approved"
API-CHECK: GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<employee_email>
test_cases.expected_result: Employee receives email notification when vacation is approved.
test_cases.notes: VacationStatusChangedEvent triggers notification on approve.

test_cases.title: Email notification on vacation reject (to employee)
test_cases.preconditions: Manager with a pending NEW vacation. Email queue cleared.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Clear email queue
2. Login as the manager and reject the vacation
3. SETUP: Trigger email sending
4. Verify email sent to the employee with rejection notice
API-CHECK: GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<employee_email>
test_cases.expected_result: Employee receives email notification when vacation is rejected.

test_cases.title: Email notification on vacation cancel (to approver)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee cancels a NEW or APPROVED vacation. Email queue cleared.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Clear email queue
2. Login as the employee
3. Cancel a vacation
4. SETUP: Trigger email sending
5. Verify email sent to the approver about cancellation
API-CHECK: GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<manager_email>
test_cases.expected_result: Approver notified when employee cancels a vacation.

test_cases.title: Pre-vacation reminder notification
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation starting in ~3 days.
SETUP: Set clock to 3 days before an approved vacation via test API.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Advance clock to 3 days before the vacation start
2. SETUP: Trigger notification job: POST /api/vacation/v1/test/vacations/notify-about-vacation
3. Verify pre-vacation email sent to the employee
4. Check email content: upcoming vacation dates, reminder
test_cases.expected_result: Pre-vacation reminder email sent N days before vacation start.
test_cases.notes: Cron job checks upcoming vacations and sends reminders.

test_cases.title: "Also notify" recipients get email notification
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation created with "Also notify" colleagues.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Clear email queue
2. Login as the employee
3. Create vacation with 1-2 colleagues in "Also notify"
4. SETUP: Trigger email sending
5. Verify emails sent to each notified colleague
API-CHECK: GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<colleague_email>
test_cases.expected_result: Each "Also notify" colleague receives email about the vacation.
test_cases.notes: Notification sent on create. vacation_notify_also table stores recipients.

test_cases.title: In-app notification badge update
test_cases.preconditions: Manager with a new pending vacation request.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Observe the navigation bar
3. Verify "Calendar of absences" badge shows count (e.g., "(28)")
4. Verify "Employees requests" sub-item shows the same or related count
5. Approve one vacation
6. Verify badge count decreases
test_cases.expected_result: Badge count reflects pending requests. Decreases after action.

test_cases.title: Sick leave created during vacation — conflict detection
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED vacation.
Sick leave capability available.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My sick leaves
3. Click "Add a sick note"
4. Set dates overlapping with the existing vacation
5. Submit the sick leave
6. Verify system handles the overlap (409 CONFLICT or auto-adjustment)
7. Check vacation status/dates for any modifications
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave overlapping vacation triggers SickLeaveCrossingVacationException (409).
test_cases.notes: SickLeaveCrossingVacationException → HTTP 409 CONFLICT. Sick leave endpoint blocked with API_SECRET_TOKEN (403).

test_cases.title: Vacation display in availability chart for team view
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with APPROVED vacation. Manager who can see chart.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Availability chart
3. Search for the team/project
4. Verify APPROVED vacation shows as green bar for the employee
5. Verify NEW/REJECTED vacations do NOT appear on chart
test_cases.expected_result: Only APPROVED and PAID vacations visible on availability chart. NEW/REJECTED excluded.

test_cases.title: Vacation impact on time report norm calculation
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with an APPROVED vacation in current report period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My tasks (report page)
3. Verify the reporting period norm accounts for vacation days
4. Verify the expected work hours are reduced by vacation days
5. Check that vacation days appear in the report period summary
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation days reduce the expected work hours norm in the report period.
test_cases.notes: Vacation days are subtracted from monthly norm calculation.

test_cases.title: Vacation display in confirmation period
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with vacation in a confirmation period. Manager with confirmation access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page
3. Find the period containing the employee's vacation
4. Verify vacation days are shown in the confirmation view
5. Verify vacation reduces the expected hours
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation page reflects vacation days in the period.

test_cases.title: Maternity leave impact — rejects NEW vacations
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee going on maternity leave.
SETUP: Trigger maternity begin event for the employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Ensure employee has a NEW vacation
2. SETUP: Trigger maternity leave begin event
3. Login as the employee
4. Navigate to My vacations
5. Verify the NEW vacation status changed to REJECTED
6. Verify current year vacation days reduced proportionally
7. Verify next-year days zeroed
test_cases.expected_result:
Maternity begin: all NEW vacations auto-rejected.
Current year days reduced proportionally. Next year days = 0.
Already-APPROVED vacations NOT rejected.
test_cases.notes: MaternityLeaveBeginEvent handler. Proportional day reduction based on remaining months.

test_cases.title: Employee sync updates vacation data
test_cases.preconditions: SETUP: Employee data changed in Company Staff system.
test_cases.steps:
1. SETUP: Trigger employee sync: POST /api/vacation/v1/test/vacations/sync
2. Verify employee office, manager, and role updates reflected
3. Check if vacation day norms updated for office changes
4. Login as the employee
5. Verify updated manager in vacation approver field
test_cases.expected_result: Employee sync updates office, manager, roles. Vacation norms recalculated if office changed.
test_cases.notes: CS sync runs on cron. Can be triggered via test API.

test_cases.title: Vacation display in planner
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with APPROVED vacation. User with planner access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as a manager with planner access
2. Navigate to Planner page
3. Find the employee in the planner grid
4. Verify vacation period is indicated in the planner
5. Verify vacation dates are visually distinct from working days
test_cases.expected_result: Planner shows vacation periods for team members.
